title: ForgeQuery
description: Forge DSL: a machine-generated, deterministically compiled query format. Never written by humans.

scan.description: Primary table. All field refs must use table.field format when joins are present.

joins.description: All joins must declare type explicitly. No default join type exists.

filter.description: Row-level filters (compiles to WHERE). Top-level items are AND-combined.

group.description: Group-by keys. Required if agg is present. Accepts column refs (string) or computed expressions {"expr":"...","as":"alias"} for date truncation, etc.

agg.description: Aggregate expressions. count and count_all are distinct functions.

having.description: Aggregate-level filters (compiles to HAVING). col must reference an agg alias, not a raw expression. Top-level items are AND-combined.

select.description: Output columns. Can be: table.col refs, agg aliases, window aliases, or computed expressions {"expr":"...","as":"alias"}.

window.description: Window function expressions (OVER clause). Aliases are referenced in select, just like agg aliases.

qualify.description: Post-window filter (like HAVING but for window results). Use to implement per-group TopN: e.g. rank <= 3. Compiles to a wrapping subquery WHERE clause.

sort.description: dir is required, no default ordering.

limit.description: Maximum rows to return. Value must come from the user's question (e.g. 'top 10' → 10).

offset.description: Rows to skip before applying LIMIT. Used for pagination (page 2 of 10 = offset 10).

explain.description: LLM intent description. Not compiled. Included in error feedback to help self-correction.

cte.description: Common Table Expressions (WITH clause). Set recursive:true on any item to emit WITH RECURSIVE.

name.description: CTE name, referenced in the main query's scan or joins.

query.description: Anchor/base Forge query for this CTE. For recursive CTEs, this is the non-recursive base case.

recursive.description: If true, this CTE is recursive. The whole WITH clause will use WITH RECURSIVE. Requires recursive_term.

recursive_term.description: The recursive part. Must reference the CTE's own name via scan or joins. Combined with query via UNION ALL (default) or UNION.

recursive_union.description: Set operation between base query and recursive_term. Defaults to union_all.

distinct.description: If true, adds DISTINCT to SELECT, deduplicating result rows. Use sparingly — DISTINCT is expensive and often indicates a missing GROUP BY.

union.description: UNION / UNION ALL with subsequent queries. The main query is the first branch. sort/limit/offset on the main query apply to the entire UNION result.

mode.description: union_all = UNION ALL (keeps duplicates, faster). union = UNION (deduplicates).

query.description: A Forge query object for this UNION branch.

intersect.description: INTERSECT with subsequent queries — returns only rows present in all branches. Column count and types must match.

query.description: A Forge query object for this INTERSECT branch.

except.description: EXCEPT with subsequent queries — subtracts rows appearing in subsequent branches from the main query result.

query.description: A Forge query object for this EXCEPT branch.

ScalarVal.description: A typed scalar, array, date value, or subquery (for IN operator).

oneOf.description: Relative date preset (resolved at query time).
Subquery for IN operator. The inner Forge query must return exactly one column.

subquery.description: A Forge query object that returns a single column.

BoundVal.description: A numeric or date value for BETWEEN lo/hi bounds.

SelectItem.description: A column reference (string) or a computed expression object.

oneOf.description: Column reference, agg alias, or window alias.
Computed expression, e.g. quantity * unit_price AS revenue.

expr.description: Arithmetic or CASE expression, passed verbatim into SQL.

type.description: anti = LEFT JOIN ... WHERE right.id IS NULL. semi = EXISTS subquery pattern.

on.description: Join condition: single equality {left, right} or multi-condition array (inner/left/right/full only).

oneOf.description: Multi-condition join (inner/left/right/full only). items are SimpleConditions AND-combined.

filter.description: Extra filter conditions on the joined table (semi/anti only). Compiled into EXISTS/NOT EXISTS subquery WHERE clause.

Condition.description: Either a simple predicate or an OR group.

col2.description: Right-hand column for col-vs-col comparison, e.g. good_count > bad_count. Use instead of val when comparing two columns directly.

AndCondition.description: AND group inside an OR. Use to express (A AND B) OR C patterns.

Aggregation.description: count_all() has no col. group_concat() has optional separator. All others require col.

col.description: Column reference or arithmetic expression, e.g. 'orders.amount' or 'order_items.quantity * order_items.unit_price'. The value is passed verbatim into the SQL function call.

filter.description: FILTER (WHERE ...) clause: only rows matching these conditions contribute to this aggregate. Example: {"fn":"count_all","as":"vip_count","filter":[{"col":"users.is_vip","op":"eq","val":1}]}. Not supported in MySQL.

AggCountAll.description: Compiles to COUNT(*). No col field.

AggGroupConcat.description: String aggregation: GROUP_CONCAT(col) or GROUP_CONCAT(col, separator). SQLite/MySQL syntax. separator defaults to ','.

separator.description: Separator string. Defaults to ',' if omitted.

WindowExpr.description: Window function. Three variants: ranking (no col), aggregate-over (col required), navigation/lag-lead (col + optional offset/default).

WindowRanking.description: Ranking/distribution functions — no input column. row_number: unique sequential integers. rank: tied rows share rank, next skips (1,1,3). dense_rank: no gap (1,1,2). percent_rank: relative rank 0..1. cume_dist: cumulative distribution 0..1. ntile(n): divides rows into n buckets (requires 'n' field).

n.description: Bucket count for ntile only. E.g. ntile(4) divides into quartiles.

WindowAgg.description: Aggregate window functions: SUM/AVG/COUNT/MIN/MAX OVER (...). Add 'frame' for moving windows (e.g. 7-day rolling average).

WindowNav.description: Navigation/value functions. lag/lead: access prior/next row's value (offset defaults to 1; default fills NULLs). first_value/last_value: first or last value in the window frame.

offset.description: lag/lead only: rows to look back/forward.

default.description: lag/lead only: value when no prior/next row exists.

frame.description: first_value/last_value: control which rows are in scope. Defaults to RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW.

WindowFrame.description: Window frame clause (ROWS/RANGE BETWEEN start AND end). Common patterns: {unit:'rows',start:'unbounded_preceding',end:'current_row'} for running total; {unit:'rows',start:'6 preceding',end:'current_row'} for 7-day rolling average.

unit.description: ROWS = physical row offset; RANGE = logical value offset (requires ORDER BY).

start.description: Frame start bound: 'unbounded_preceding', 'current_row', or 'N preceding' / 'N following'.

end.description: Frame end bound: 'current_row', 'unbounded_following', or 'N preceding' / 'N following'. If omitted, the frame is a single-bound expression.

GroupExpr.description: Computed GROUP BY expression with optional alias. Use for date truncation grouping: {"expr":"STRFTIME('%Y-%m', orders.created_at)","as":"month"}. The alias is referenced in select.

expr.description: SQL expression for GROUP BY (e.g. STRFTIME, DATE_TRUNC, CAST).

as.description: Alias to reference this computed group key in select.
